docs.title: About digital waste tracking
docs.text:
The Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (Defra) intend to transform how data about waste movements is collected and shared with the regulators across all 4 UK nations. Rollout of a new digital service is planned from April 2026.

You can read more about the digital waste tracking policy on GOV.UK. To get the latest updates from Defra on circular economy reforms (including waste tracking), sign up to receive our newsletter.

docs.title: Submitting data about waste movements: planned changes
docs.text:
We are exploring the development of new APIs to integrate with waste management software and other systems. By having waste carriers and receivers submit data via APIs, we can give regulators a more joined-up and comprehensive picture of all waste movements.

This site will contain draft details for the technical design of the service, including a list of data definitions, and a link to the draft API specification.

docs.title: How we are engaging with the waste industry
docs.text:
We want to test the feasibility of an API-driven service as early as we can, including learning about any barriers to delivering it. We welcome feedback from people in the waste industry to help us do this.

We are currently talking to the following groups of people to help us learn about any barriers to us delivering this new service:

  • waste carriers
  • waste receivers
  • waste management software suppliers

docs.title: How to get involved
docs.text:
We are planning further user research and other opportunities for people in the waste industry to give feedback and test the solutions we're looking to develop. To register an interest in taking part, sign up for the waste tracking user panel.

Page last updated on 16 May 2025.

docs.title: Draft data definitions (work in progress)
docs.text:
These draft data definition tables describe the information we expect to be recorded by the future waste tracking service when a:

  • carrier collects waste
  • receiving site accepts or rejects waste

They are a work in progress and may be updated to reflect changes to policy, legislation and feedback received from those in the waste industry.

docs.title: Collection data (when a carrier collects waste)
Waste movement, producer and collection details
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 1 Unique reference ID Optional This could be a unique weighbridge ticket number, waste transfer note number etc. 2 Waste source Mandatory This is the origin or subsequent place where the waste was produced. This should be either the commercial (business) or household address where the waste was produced. 3 Special handling requirement Optional Required only for abnormal hazardous waste that might contain harmful chemical, biological or physical characteristics. These may require some special handling to prevent harm. 4 Producer organisation name Mandatory Name of the business or organisation that produced the waste. 5 Producer Companies House registration number Optional Applies only to companies registered with Companies House. Producer’s Companies House registration number is a unique number that identifies a company and verifies its legal status. 6 Site waste authorisation number they are operating under Mandatory This is a type of permit that could be held by a commercial waste producer. 7 Producer address Mandatory The producer’s address. 8 Producer email address Mandatory (optional if telephone number is provided) The producer’s email address. 9 Producer contact telephone number Mandatory (optional if email address is provided) Required if email address is not available or provided. 10 What is the SIC code for the process that created this waste? Mandatory The Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifies the type of business activity that generated the waste. 11 Is producer address same as waste collection address? Mandatory ‘Yes’ or ‘no’. 12 Waste collection address Optional if above is yes Only required if different to where the waste was produced. 13 Are producer contact details same as collection contact details? Mandatory ‘Yes’ or ‘no’. 14 Waste collection contact details (if different) Optional if above is yes Only required if different to those of the producer.

docs.title: Waste type
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 15 EWC code (could be multiple EWC codes for one load) Mandatory An EWC Code is a 6 digit code listed in the European Waste Catalogue (EWC). The EWC is a list of waste types which categorises waste based on a combination of what they are, and the process or activity that produces them. 16 Waste description (proper description of actual waste not EWC description) Optional A detailed description of a specific waste material, including its physical characteristics, composition, and any potential hazards associated with it. It's essentially a classification of the waste that helps determine how it should be managed and disposed of safely. 17 Waste form Mandatory This is the form of the waste. This could be a gas, liquid, solid, powder, sludge or mixed etc. 18 Number & type of containers Mandatory Containers are receptacles used for storing, transporting, and disposing of waste materials. They come in various sizes and types, ranging from small desk-side bins to large industrial skips. 19 Waste quantity (what metric e.g. kg, tonnes etc.)? Mandatory Total weight of the waste (typically in kilograms or tonnes). 20 Is the waste quantity estimate or actual? Mandatory Specifies whether the quantity of waste is estimated or actual. 21 Does the waste contain hazardous properties? Y/N Mandatory Waste is generally considered to contain hazardous properties if it (or the material or substances it contains) is harmful to humans or the environment. E.g. asbestos, batteries, solvents etc. 22 Does the waste contain persistent organic pollutants (POPs)? Y/N (Could be multiple POPs for each EWC code) Mandatory Persistent organic pollutants (POPs) are chemical substances that do not break down in the environment. They are a danger to human health and the environment. 23 POP name Mandatory E.g. Aldrin, Chlordane, Dieldrin etc. 24 POP concentration value Mandatory E.g. 50mg per kg of Chlordane. 25 Chemical or biological component Mandatory E.g. Mercury. 26 Component concentration value Mandatory E.g. 30mg per kg. 27 Hazardous property code Mandatory Hazard property (HP) codes are used to classify hazardous waste. E.g. HP 1 - Explosive Waste, HP 2 - Oxidising Waste, HP 3 - Flammable Waste etc. Waste classification technical guidance - GOV.UK

docs.title: Carrier details
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 28 Carrier registration number Mandatory A waste carrier registration number is a legal requirement for businesses that transport, buy, sell, or dispose of waste. This license ensures that waste is handled and transported in a responsible and environmentally friendly manner. 29 Driver number ID (if applicable) Optional This is a driver identification number. 30 Carrier organisation name Mandatory The business name of the carrier. 31 Carrier address Mandatory The business location address of the carrier. 32 Carrier contact email address Mandatory The email address of the carrier. 33 Companies House number Mandatory Carrier’s Companies House registration number is a unique number that identifies a company and verifies its legal status. 34 Carrier contact phone number Mandatory The waste carrier business contact number. 35 Vehicle registration number Mandatory The vehicle registration number of the carrier. 36 Means of transport (road, rail, air, sea, inland waterway, others) Mandatory This describes how the waste is transported.

docs.title: Receiver’s details and waste collection (receipt) declaration
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 37 Receiver’s organisation name Mandatory This is the registered name of the waste receiving site. 38 Receiver's address Mandatory This is the business location address of the receiving site. 39 Companies House number Mandatory Receiver’s Companies House registration number is a unique number that identifies a company and verifies its legal status. 40 Receiver’s contact email Mandatory (optional if phone number is provided) This is the receiving site email address. 41 Receiver’s contact phone number Mandatory (optional if email address is provided) This is the receiving site contact phone number. 42 Receiver’s type of site waste authorisation Mandatory Waste sites have permits or licenses for certain types of waste. E.g. it could be a landfill site. 43 Receiver’s authorisation number Mandatory This is the site's authorisation (permit or exemption) number that allows it to accept waste for intended recovery and disposal operation. 44 Regulatory position statement Mandatory A regulatory position statement (RPS) is issued when the Environment Agency don’t require you to have a permit to carry out certain activities that they regulate. The statement will state which activities you do not require a permit for. 45 Date / time waste was received Mandatory This is the date and exact time waste was received at the site. 46 Estimate or actual tonnage? Mandatory Is it estimated or actual waste tonnage? 47 Disposal / recovery code, for each EWC (choose one or more R/D codes per EWC and weight/quantity for each code) Mandatory Waste disposal and recovery codes are for categorising waste and determining the most appropriate treatment and recovery options. 48 Waste collection declaration Mandatory A tick box or message to show waste collected.

docs.title: Receipt data (when a site accepts waste)
Waste receipt confirmation
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 1 Unique reference ID Optional This could be a unique weighbridge ticket number, waste transfer note number etc. 2 Special handling requirement Optional Required only for abnormal hazardous waste that might contain harmful chemical, biological or physical characteristics. These may require some special handling to prevent harm. 3 All waste movement IDs being received (multiple in the case of multiple collections) Optional Waste Movement ID. 4 EWC code (could be multiple EWC codes for one load) Mandatory An EWC code is a 6 digit code listed in the European Waste Catalogue (EWC). The EWC is a list of waste types which categorises waste based on a combination of what they are, and the process or activity that produces them. 5 Waste description (proper description of actual waste not EWC description) Optional A detailed description of a specific waste material, including its physical characteristics, composition, and any potential hazards associated with it. It's essentially a classification of the waste that helps determine how it should be managed and disposed of safely. 6 Waste form Mandatory This is the form of the waste. This could be a gas, liquid, solid, powder, sludge or mixed etc. 7 Number & type of containers Mandatory Containers are receptacles used for storing, transporting, and disposing of waste materials. They come in various sizes and types, ranging from small desk-side bins to large industrial skips. 8 Waste quantity (what metric e.g kg, tonnes etc.)? Mandatory Total weight of the waste (typically in kilograms or tonnes). 9 Is the waste quantity estimate or actual? Mandatory Specifies whether the quantity of waste is estimated or actual. 10 Does the waste contain hazardous properties? Y/N Mandatory Waste is generally considered to contain hazardous properties if it (or the material or substances it contains) are harmful to humans or the environment. E.g. asbestos, batteries, solvents etc. 11 Does the waste contain persistent organic pollutants (POPs)? Y/N (could be multiple POPs for each EWC code) Mandatory Persistent organic pollutants (POPs) are chemical substances that do not break down in the environment. They are a danger to human health and the environment. 12 POP name Mandatory E.g. Aldrin, Chlordane, Dieldrin etc. 13 POP concentration value Mandatory E.g. 50mg per kg of Chlordane. 14 Chemical or biological component Mandatory E.g. Mercury. 15 Component concentration value Mandatory E.g. 30mg per kg. 16 Hazardous property code Mandatory Hazard property (HP) codes are used to classify hazardous waste. E.g. HP 1 - Explosive Waste, HP 2 - Oxidising Waste, HP 3 - Flammable Waste etc. Waste classification technical guidance - GOV.UK

docs.title: Carrier details
docs.text: Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 17 Are you accepting all the waste? Mandatory Is any of the waste rejected? 18 If no, what quantity of the waste are you not accepting? Mandatory Quantity of waste in kg or % not accepted. 19 Reason(s) for not accepting all the waste if not accepted? Mandatory What are the reasons why all or part of the waste is rejected? 20 Carrier registration number Mandatory A waste carrier registration number is a legal requirement for businesses that transport, buy, sell, or dispose of waste. This license ensures that waste is handled and transported in a responsible and environmentally friendly manner. 21 Driver number ID  (if applicable) Optional This is a driver identification number. 22 Carrier organisation name Mandatory The business name of the carrier. 23 Carrier address Mandatory The business location address of the carrier. 24 Carrier contact email address Mandatory The email address of the carrier. 25 Companies House number Mandatory Carrier’s Companies House registration number is a unique number that identifies a company and verifies its legal status. 26 Carrier contact phone number Mandatory The waste carrier business contact number. 27 Vehicle registration number Mandatory The vehicle registration number of the carrier. 28 Means of transport (road, rail, air, sea, inland waterway, others) Mandatory This describes how the waste is transported.

docs.title: Receiver's details and waste collection (receipt) declaration
docs.text:
Number Data field Mandatory or optional Description 29 Receiver’s organisation name Mandatory This is the registered name of the waste receiving site. 30 Receiver's address Mandatory This is the business location address of the receiving site. 31 Companies House number Mandatory Receiver’s Companies House registration number is a unique number that identifies a company and verifies its legal status. 32 Receiver’s contact email Mandatory (optional if phone number is provided) This is the receiving site email address. 33 Receiver’s contact phone number Mandatory (optional if email address is provided) This is the receiving site contact phone number. 34 Receiver’s type of site waste authorisation Mandatory Waste sites have permits or licenses for certain types of waste. E.g. it could be a landfill site. 35 Receiver’s authorisation number Mandatory This is the site's authorisation (permit or exemption) number that allows it to accept waste for intended recovery and disposal operation. 36 Regulatory position statement Mandatory A regulatory position statement (RPS) is issued when the Environment Agency don’t require you to have a permit to carry out certain activities that they regulate. The statement will state which activities you do not require a permit for. 37 Date / time waste was received Mandatory This is the date and exact time waste was received at the site. 38 Estimate or actual tonnage? Mandatory Is it estimated or actual waste tonnage? 39 Disposal / recovery code, for each EWC (choose one or more R/D codes per EWC and weight/quantity for each code) Mandatory Waste disposal and recovery codes are for categorising waste and determining the most appropriate treatment and recovery options. 40 Waste receipt declaration Mandatory A tick box or message to show waste was received.

Page last updated on 16 May 2025.
